name: Branches of Physics
description: Physics is huge. So physicists divide it into branches to focus on different kinds of stuff.

content.overview: Physics covers everything from atoms to planets to lightning to your phone — too much for one field to handle at once. So physicists split it into branches, the same way biology splits into zoology and botany. Each branch focuses on one kind of behavior. {{viz:diagram-01}}
keyPoints: Physics is divided into branches so physicists can focus on specific areas
Classical mechanics = how things move and interact
Electricity & Magnetism = charged particles and magnets
Quantum mechanics = atoms and tiny particles
Modern physics = extreme speeds and forces
content.whyItMatters: Knowing the branches tells you where to look for an answer. A question about motion points to classical mechanics. A question about atoms points to quantum mechanics. A question about your charger points to electricity and magnetism. It's the difference between guessing and knowing exactly which tool to reach for.
